rent.note: furnished 1BR in Centro Histórico or Juriquilla; range $500–$900

utilities.note: electric + water + fiber internet; mild climate keeps A/C moderate

groceries.note: home cooking; mercados + Walmart/Costco present as industrial city

diningOut.note: comidas corridas + nice restaurants; dining scene growing

transit.note: buses + Uber; good connections to CDMX/Bajío

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; tech/industrial city has solid coworking options

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base.

evidence.quote: A single person's costs total approximately $1,145 per month, while monthly budgets average $1,300–$1,600

evidence.claim: avg rent ~$538/month; 1BR $500–$900 in popular expat areas
evidence.quote: Monthly rent costs approximately $538 per month, though a one-bedroom apartment in popular expat cities like Querétaro typically runs $500 to $900 USD

evidence.claim: 25–30% cheaper than CDMX; fast-growing tech hub
evidence.quote: Querétaro is a fast-growing industrial and tech hub with costs 25%–30% lower than Mexico City, making it an attractive destination for remote workers

evidence.claim: realistic nomad budget $1,000–$1,500 with strong urban services
evidence.quote: Querétaro offers the best value for many nomads, with realistic monthly budgets of $1,000–$1,500 USD and strong urban services plus direct bus access to Mexico City
